title: Teaching a Student to Skip Steps
concept: Distillation (callback to knowledge distillation concepts) trains a smaller/faster 'student' model to mimic a slow, many-step 'teacher' diffusion model.

exercises.prompt: If a slow chef takes 50 steps to bake a perfect cake, what would be helpful for a student chef to learn?

options.text: A faster way to get almost the same cake in fewer steps
How to bake a completely different cake
How to make the cake take even longer

exercises.explain: That's the idea behind distillation! We train a 'student' to get similar great results, but much faster.

cards.title: What if AI could skip the boring parts?
cards.body: Some AI image-makers (diffusion models) need 50+ slow steps to turn noise into a picture. What if we could teach a new AI to do it in just 1-4 steps, almost as well? That's exactly what distillation does!

cards.title: Distillation
cards.body: Distillation means training a smaller, faster 'student' model to copy the results of a slow, many-step 'teacher' model. The student learns to jump straight to a good answer instead of taking every single step the teacher took.

cards.title: The Marathon Runner's Shortcut
cards.body: Imagine a wise old runner who knows every twist of a 50-mile trail and always finishes perfectly, but slowly. Now imagine training a young runner by showing them exactly where the trail ends up — so they can sprint straight there, skipping most of the winding path, and still arrive in almost the same great shape.

cards.title: Fast Image Generators
cards.body: A teacher diffusion model might need 50 steps to draw a cat picture from noise. A distilled student model watches the teacher's final results and learns to draw a very similar cat in just 1-4 steps — making apps feel instant instead of slow.

cards.title: Myth: The student just copies pixels
cards.body: It's not simple copy-pasting! The student model learns the general pattern of how to jump from noisy input to a good final result, so it can create new images too — just much faster than the teacher, and without needing all the in-between steps.

cards.title: Teacher vs. Student Model
cards.body: The teacher takes many small careful steps. The student learns to leap to a similar result in far fewer steps.

left.text: Slow but thorough — 50+ steps to remove noise bit by bit

right.text: Fast and nearly as good — learns to jump to the result in 1-4 steps

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of distillation in diffusion models?

options.text: To make the teacher model slower
To train a faster student model that mimics the teacher's results
To delete the teacher model completely
To add more steps to the generation process

exercises.explain: Distillation trains a smaller, faster student model to produce results similar to the slow teacher model, but in far fewer steps.

exercises.prompt: A distilled student model usually needs MORE steps than the original teacher model.
exercises.explain: False! The whole point of distillation is that the student needs far fewer steps (like 1-4) instead of the teacher's 50+ steps.

exercises.sentence: In distillation, a fast ___ model learns to mimic a slow teacher model.
exercises.explain: The 'student' model is trained to copy the teacher's results but much faster, using far fewer steps.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes what the student model learns from the teacher?

options.text: How to jump straight to a similar final result, skipping most steps
How to take even more steps than the teacher
How to copy the teacher's exact pixel-by-pixel process

exercises.explain: The student learns the general shortcut pattern from noisy input to good output, not the exact step-by-step process.

exercises.prompt: A company wants their AI art app to generate images instantly on phones, instead of taking 30 seconds like their current 50-step model. What should they do?

options.text: Use distillation to train a fast student model that mimics the teacher in just a few steps
Add more steps to the model to make it more careful
Make the teacher model even slower for better quality
Remove the AI model completely and use random noise

exercises.explain: Distillation is perfect for this! Training a student model lets the app generate similar-quality images in just 1-4 steps instead of 50+, making it fast enough for phones.
